Progent.goal: Controls unsafe LLM-agent tool use through programmable privilege control.
Progent.method: Defines runtime policies over tool calls and arguments using a structured policy language enforced deterministically.
Progent.key_contribution: Applies least-privilege style control to agent tool usage at runtime.
Progent.main_limitation: Still depends on policy coverage and correct policy specification.

MCP-Secure.goal: Improves safety of MCP-based tool access for LLM systems.
MCP-Secure.method: Uses scoped access, read-only defaults, and approval-gated privilege elevation for tool interactions.
MCP-Secure.key_contribution: Adds host-side control over tool permissions and session behavior.
MCP-Secure.main_limitation: May trade off convenience and flexibility for stronger control.

AgentSpec.goal: Constrains unsafe agent behavior through runtime rules.
AgentSpec.method: Uses a lightweight rule language with triggers, predicates, and enforcement actions.
AgentSpec.key_contribution: Makes runtime safety rules explicit and enforceable during agent execution.
AgentSpec.main_limitation: Runtime checks may not fully capture long-horizon or future-state consequences.

Conseca.goal: Generates context-sensitive security policies for agents.
Conseca.method: Builds just-in-time contextual policies from trusted context and enforces them deterministically.
Conseca.key_contribution: Adapts policies to the current task context rather than using one fixed rule set.
Conseca.main_limitation: Policy quality depends on the trusted context and policy-generation process.

AgentGuardian.goal: Learns security controls for agent workflows from benign traces.
AgentGuardian.method: Uses staging-phase traces to derive policies and govern runtime workflow behavior.
AgentGuardian.key_contribution: Combines learned controls with workflow-level runtime governance.
AgentGuardian.main_limitation: Coverage depends on how representative the benign traces are.

AgentArmor.goal: Analyzes agent execution traces to detect unsafe behavior.
AgentArmor.method: Treats runtime traces as structured programs and inspects dependence relationships for security violations.
AgentArmor.key_contribution: Provides graph-based runtime analysis of agent behavior.
AgentArmor.main_limitation: Its effectiveness depends on trace representation and analysis coverage.

ACE.goal: Secures LLM-integrated app systems by separating planning and execution.
ACE.method: Uses abstract planning, concrete planning, isolated execution, and static information-flow controls.
ACE.key_contribution: Places strong security boundaries around application execution and information flow.
ACE.main_limitation: Its guarantees depend on the architecture and accuracy of boundary enforcement.
